activities_projects.title: Types of Farming Identification
steps: Teacher explains different types of farming such as subsistence and commercial farming
Students list examples of farming practices
Classify them into subsistence or commercial farming
Discuss their importance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify different types of farming methods
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification of farming types (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Crop Season Chart
activities_projects.objective: Learn about crop seasons in India
steps: Teacher explains Kharif, Rabi, and Zaid crop seasons
Students prepare a chart showing crops grown in each season
Add examples like rice, wheat, and vegetables
Display the chart in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the different crop seasons

activities_projects.title: Map Activity: Major Crops of India
activities_projects.objective: Locate major crop growing regions in India
steps: Provide a blank map of India
Mark areas where rice, wheat, and cotton are grown
Label each region clearly
Discuss why these crops grow in those regions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn the geographical distribution of crops

activities_projects.title: Poster on Importance of Agriculture
steps: Students create posters about the importance of agriculture
Include drawings of farmers and crops
Add slogans related to farming
Display posters in the classroom
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of agriculture in daily life
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance of poster (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion on Modern Farming
steps: Divide the class into groups
Discuss modern techniques like irrigation and fertilizers
List advantages of modern farming
Present ideas to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how technology improves agriculture
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Farm Visit or Observation
activities_projects.objective: Observe farming practices in real life
steps: Students observe nearby farms or farming activities
Note types of crops grown
Observe farming tools used
Write observations in a notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect textbook knowledge with real-life farming

activities_projects.title: Seed Collection Activity
steps: Students collect seeds of crops like rice, wheat, and pulses
Label each seed type
Discuss which crop each seed belongs to
Create a small seed collection display

activities_projects.title: Reading and Reflection on Food Production
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of agriculture in food supply
steps: Read the section on food production
Identify major food crops
Write a short paragraph about how agriculture supports people
Discuss answers in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of agriculture in food security

activities_projects.title: Role Play on Farmer’s Life
steps: Assign roles such as farmer, trader, and consumer
Discuss problems faced by farmers
Suggest possible solutions
Act out the scenario
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand agricultural challenges and solutions

activities_projects.title: Project: Agriculture Around Us
activities_projects.objective: Study crops grown in the local area
steps: Identify crops grown in the local region
Collect information about planting and harvesting seasons
Prepare a small report
Present the findings in class
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of information and presentation (10 marks)
